name: Adderstrike Venom
process: Pick apart the caps of the Night Prizes and place them in a vial. Cover just slightly with water and stir gently for a Quick 30. Chop up the stems with a sharp object and slowly add to the mixture. Let it boil for three full hourglass turns and then decant into a vial or jar. Deposit Night Prizes into Ashbin.
description: A reddish brown liquid that slightly burns to the touch when applied to bare skin. It has a faint acidic smell to it.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack with a +2 bonus to its damage “by Poison” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Adderstrike Venom will become inert. This may also be added to a drink and it will cause 4 points of Piercing damage by Poison.

name: Alcohol
process: Grind the Bloom into a fine powder, and mix with water inside the retort. Heat the retort with a flame, allow the solution to boil and collect the final product.
description: Most ancient of all the alchemy is the sacred creation of alcohol. With but a single Bloom and other ingredients for flavoring and texture, three servings of this mystical brew can be created. Each serving is the approximate strength of three shots of most alcohols.
effect: This brew only has role-playing effects. It will induce a role-playing effect of being Intoxicated. The player may role-play this as they wish. The character can remain drunk until she completes a Long Rest; however, the player may opt to end it at any time for whatever reason. Its effects may be removed by Cure or Dispel Poison.

name: Alchemical Suspension
process: Take the Resource and meditate over it for a Quick 30 before running water over it and catch the runoff for Suspension.
description: A clear Alchemical Suspension.
effect: Produces a finite amount of Alchemical Suspension, enough to fill any standard container. This container of suspension will provide enough fluid for any number of crafts in any given gathering, but generally expires at the end of the event.

name: Alchemical Salts

effect: Produces a finite amount of salts, enough to fill any standard container. This container of salts will provide enough salt for any number of crafts in any given gathering, but generally expires at the end of the event.

name: Bloom Powder
process: Take Bloom five at a time and grind them in the mortal and pestle for 3 turns of the hourglass. Take the resulting powder and bottle it. After this process is complete, place the Bloom in the Ashbin and fill out the Bloom Powder card.
description: A small vial with brightly colored powder that looks like colored sand, often the same color as the petals of the flowers used to make it.
effect: Bloom Powder may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Bloom such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Bloom Powder to make additional Bloom Powder or other crafting that requires Bloom physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.

name: Daggerpoint Cognac
process: Take two Bloom and dice them into fine pieces. Take the diced Bloom and add it to a beaker. Fill the beaker 3/4 of the way with water. Heat the beaker over the flame for 2 turns of the hourglass. Blow onto the beaker to cool it for about 10 breaths. Transfer the liquid contents into another vial or beaker, while straining out the pieces of Bloom the best you can. Discard the pieces of Bloom that you just strained out. Add 2 pinches of Simple Salts to the remaining liquid. Place the other two Bloom whole into the beaker and heat for another turn of the hourglass. Stir halfway through. Remove the bloom, and decant the liquid into a vial.
description: This crimson colored cognac smells vaguely of blackcurrant.
effect: This small dose of strong tasting liquor is enough to send a shiver down the spine of those who drink it, and quite possibly those who fall victim to those who drink it. When drunk, the Rogue Refreshes one Spike, as long as they have the Backstab power. Usable once per Short Rest.

name: Essence Infusion
process: Grind the Rare Minerals in the mortar and add to a vial of Alchemical Suspension. Cut up the Harvest and add to the vial. Gently stir over low heat for one turn of the hourglass. The Infusion is now complete.
description: A clear substance with strange swirls in it. Staring intently at the swirls will bring thoughts to mind that match the Essence held within.
effect: The Essence Infusion is what is used when Alchemical Recipes, Tinkering Schematics, Rituals, or Enchanting Formulae calls for an Essence. It is the vessel that enchanters store and transfer Essence Crafting Materials that they draw from the Reality Tear. In order to store an Essence, an enchanter holds the vial in one hand, with another touching the Reality Tear. They will then focus on the Essence that is within their soulhold for a Quick 100. Once that is complete, the Essence Infusion now contains the same energy that was within their soulhold.

name: Hardening Lacquer
process: Place the Rare Minerals in a vial. Heat the Ingot over the flame, holding it in the tongs. After one turn of the hourglass, scrape shavings off the Ingots with the knife into the Rare Minerals vial. Pour the mixture into the mortar and add four drops of Alchemical Suspension. Grind into a paste. Scoop back into a vial and cork.
description: This dark paste has flecks of sparkle in it and it is usually applied with a disposable rag.
effect: This special lacquer can be rubbed vigorously on any item to give it a Protect vs Shatter. If the lacquered item is handed around, the information card must go with it. If ingested, the Hardening Lacquer causes Drain by Poison.

name: Healing Draught
process: Take the Bloom and fit it entirely into a small vial. Add three drops of water and shake the vial vigorously for one hourglass turn. Then, let stand for a second hourglass turn. Finish the product by adding Alchemical Suspension until the vial is full.
description: A dark gold liquid, it smells slightly of spirits, citrus and peaches.
effect: Heals 1 Life Point upon drinking the potion.

name: Instant Glue
process: Stretch out the hide and cut it into two strips about 2 by 6 each. Set aside. Take the Night Prize and pull it apart and place it into a beaker. Take two pinches of Simple Salts and spread over the Night Prize. Leave it for one turn of the hourglass. Use the eyedropper to add a drop of Alchemical Suspension, one at a time, until the night prizes are a soft gooey mess (about five drops). Stir and massage the mixture for one turn of the hourglass. Lay out each strip of leather, one on top of each other forming an X. Use Night Prize mixture as glue to attach the leather strips to each other, making a little ball shaped pouch. Put the rest of the Night Prize mixture inside the pouch and seal it, using the mixture as glue. Once finished it should be a small leather ball filled with a sticky glue.
description: This recipe produces a thin bag filled with a viscous fluid that hardens immediately on contact.
effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to cause Quick Root by Poison. If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill, Thrown Weapons, or Apprentice Alchemist skills, that character takes the Effect.

fields.Requirements: Thrown Weapons or Poisoner or Apprentice Alchemy

name: Nyte Tea
process: Take the Night Prize and add it to a hot cup of water. Let it steep for 2 turns. Remove it from the water and tear it into smaller pieces. After letting it cool, add it back into the water. Decant into another vial and let sit for 2 more turns.
description: A light tea, with a slight tingling upon the tongue when drunk. Brewers often have their own blends that alter and add brighter flavors to the drink. Many different blends are enjoyed by different cultures around the land, and everyone seems to have their favorite.
effect: This brew only has role-playing effects. It will induce a very slight hallucinogenic buzz, much like alcohol-intoxication. The character may experience mild sensory hallucinations of a generically pleasant kind. Too much Tea can result in darker and more revealing sensory experiences. The player may role-play this as they wish. The effect will last until the character completes a Long Rest. However, the player may opt to end it at any time for whatever reason. Its effects can also be removed by Cure or Dispel Poison.

name: Salve of Shielding
process: Start with taking the petals off the bloom. Deposit them into a small beaker. Fill with water, about 1/4 of the way. Place over a flame and heat for 3 turns of the hourglass. While it is heating up, chop up the stem and add it in slowly while stirring the liquid. After it is finished heating, take two pinches of Simple Salts and add to the mixture. It should quickly thicken up. Mix it well, for about a turn of an hourglass. Transfer the salve to another container for final use.
description: This cream colored salve refreshes a Mage’s ability to grant Barrier to others.
effect: This salve when applied refreshes a Mage's Arcane Barrier if it is currently not available to be cast.

name: Simple Ink
process: Carefully soak the leaves or flowers of the Bloom in water for three turns of the hourglass. Discard any other pieces of the Bloom you might have. Squeeze out all liquid and press the damp leaves and/or flowers into a very thin sheet. Take the dropper and place 8 drops of Alchemical Suspension onto the sheet, as spread out as possible. Mash into a paste using the Ingot, making sure to use all sides of the Ingot. Discard the used Ingot. Bottle the paste and fill to the top with water. Gently shake the container for a few seconds.
description: Simple Ink is inherently a reddish color.
effect: This creates a batch of ink that Enchanters can use to scribe scrolls. It makes up to two spells worth of ink that can be used for Novice scrolls (only). See Enchanting for more information on scribing scrolls.

name: Vial of Transformation
process: Grind up the Night Prize in the mortar and pestle. Once thoroughly muddled, add 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension. Set aside. Lightly cha each Bloom over the flame for one turn of the hourglass. Pick apart the Bloom and place into a pile. Take the Night Prize mixture and slowly drop it into the middle of the pile of charred bloom. Slowly fold in the bloom for two turns of the hourglass making sure it is thoroughly mixed. Add the mixture to a vial and fill with water. Shake vigorously to make sure it is combined. Drain out the muddy liquid into a new vial and discard the remains of the Bloom and Night Prizes.
description: This muddy, dark liquid contains natural power - such that a reasonably attuned magic user might find themselves empowered to transform themselves once again.
effect: This creates a vial of liquid that when ingested, refreshes a spell-slot of the character’s highest known Tier. This spell slot can only be used to cast a Form spell. The user may only ingest one Vial of Transformation per Long Rest before its effects instead become toxic, causing the user to become nauseated with no benefit.

name: Absolution
process: Using the knife, shave down the Hides as much as possible. Grind the Bloom, Harvest, and contents of a spell-packet/materia (a spell-packet should not actually be disassembled) in the mortar. Mix with water, adding drop by drop until a stiff paste is created. Let sit for three turns of the hourglass and then grind up into powder in the mortar.
description: This honey-thick gray liquid tastes about how you might expect it would. It has a faint scent of ash and cinder.
effect: Absolution cures the lingering effects from Corrupted Alchemy. After ingesting Absolution, the character is cured of Corrupting Alchemy’s lingering effects (Inherent Weakness, and “reduce all sources of Healing to 1 point until cured.”) This alchemy has no effect on creatures that quaff it who are not affected by Corrupted Alchemy.This curative for Corrupted Alchemy is a short-lived alchemical brew that cures the effects of Corrupted Alchemy, however, it comes with the side-effect of making future Corrupted Alchemy less potent. For each Absolution that character takes over the course of the Event, Corrupted Alchemy takes twice the normal doses to have any effect. This drawback stacks upon itself. All stacks of Absolution wear off at the end of the event.

name: Arcane Ink
materials: 2 Bloom, 1 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 4 Night Prizes
process: Take the Harvest and dice into small pieces. Rip up the Bloom and add to the Harvest. Add three drops of water to the Harvest pile. Take the Night Prizes and grind in the mortar and pestle. Mix the ground Night Prizes by hand with the Harvest. Add the mixture above with the Rare Minerals and bring to boil in a vial for two turns of the hourglass. Discard all non-liquid ingredients and decant the liquid into vial.
description: A brownish color, Arcane Ink has a faint golden sheen that sparkles in the sun.
effect: This creates a batch of ink that Enchanters can use to scribe scrolls. It makes enough ink to pen two scrolls that can scribe Novice or Adept-tier spells.

name: Bloodseeker Elixir
process: This liquid must sit for at least seven hours to ferment. Take the four Night Prizes and add to the bottom of a vial or container. Fill halfway with water. Prick a finger and add 5 drops of blood to the draught. Slowly warm over heat for two turns of the hourglass uncorked. Quickly remove from heat and cork the mixture. Let the mixture rest for at least seven hours to ferment. The longer it ferments, the stronger the intoxication effect.
description: A strong heady drink, mildly alcoholic with a faint metallic taste.
effect: After the character drinks this potion, it will increase the Maximum number of Bonus Spikes a character has by +3 until the end of the next Short Rest. It will also create a mild intoxication effect during this period that can end at any time the player wishes.

name: Greater Healing Draught
process: Take all five Bloom and fit entirely into a vial. Add nine drops of water and shake the vial vigorously for one hourglass’s turn. Then, let it stand for a second turn. Finish the product by adding Alchemical Suspension until the vial is full.
description: A green-tinted liquid that smells strongly of spirits, citrus and peaches. This is often just called a “Health Potion.
effect: Heal 8 upon drinking the potion.

name: Elixir of Expeditious Retreat
process: Take the Harvest and soak it in three finger-widths of water. While it soaks, heat up both Bloom over a candle until the edges of the Bloom start to crisp. Remove the spent Harvest from the water and discard into the Ashbin. Take the Bloom and muddle within the water that the Harvest had been soaking in. Heat the entire mixture over a candle for one turn of the hourglass. Then quickly bottle the preparation and stopper. Let cool in a bottle for use.
description: Once opened, the clear substance begins to react with the air, heating up. When drunk, the heat consumes the mouth for a few quick seconds but eventually fades as it wears off.
effect: Immediately after quaffing this elixir, the character gains Immunity to 1-point Weapon attacks (including Uncalled Damage) and Resistance to Damage and Effects that cause Conditions. This Immunity is very specific however. It will only last until the character makes an attack, uses any Power, spends more than 2 seconds without taking a step, or until the character has taken ten steps.

name: Enervation Poison
process: Wear gloves for this process, or you could accidentally poison yourself! Heat the Night Prizes slowly over a candle flame until it is hot to the touch (about 30 seconds), but do not burn. Squeeze the warm Night Prizes over a vial and collect any liquid. Add a drop of iron from ore-grade dirt or a drop of your blood to determine the potency; if it bubbles, the process was successful.
description: A purplish sticky liquid that smells slightly of citrus. The liquid easily sticks to whatever it is poured on and is difficult to wipe off.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that causes “Weakness by Poison” before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Enervation Poison will become inert. When ingested, Enervation Poison causes Drain by Poison.

name: Extract Hyperium
process: Place the Rare Minerals in the mortar with a sprinkle of Simple Salts and vigorously grind with the pestle for two full turns of the hourglass. Once grinding begins, it is imperative that a continuous motion is kept up until a fine powder is formed. Then, place the ground dust within a Small Vial and heat over the flame. Two drops of Alchemical suspension should be added and allowed to burn off. This process should take one half turn of the hourglass. Set aside and allow to cool. More than one turn of heating at this point ruins the materials, and needs to be begun again.Grind the Bloom and Night Prizes within the Mortar until a fine paste is formed and add four pinches of Simple Salts. Once a thick paste has been formed, combine the ground materials and the paste and hold over the flame for two full turns of the hourglass swirling occasionally. Allow the paste to char and solidify until white crystals begin to form. After crystallization occurs, remove and allow to cool, leaving unrefined Hyperium as silvery white crystal on a darkened slag brick.
description: Unrefined Hyperium is a silvery-white crystal that can be extracted from Rare minerals with a lengthy process. Unrefined Hyperium is very delicate, so often, it is kept in a brick of bloom and night-prize slag to keep it supported while additional refining processes are being planned.
effect: Begins the process of creating Hyperium, a rare resource.

name: Holy Oil
process: While speaking a soft chant specific to your Devotion’s tenets, take the Bloom, grind it, and mix it into a vial of water. Then, heat it slowly over a candle flame for five hourglass turns while swirling. Once it begins to simmer, remove the vial, and allow it to cool. Now, either remove the Bloom via decanting or straining the remaining liquid, which will become the final product.
description: A clear oil, with a faint floral scent. Looking closely at it, you can see particles within it that slightly glimmer.
effect: Upon pouring the oil over a packet of materia, the materia can then be thrown to harm the Undead. The Call for this packet is “Piercing 2 to Undead by Divine. If applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, it allows the character to add +2 damage to a Spike the character uses as long as they add the “by Divine” Accent and the “to Undead” Qualifier. If ingested (undead hunters might lace bodies with Holy Oil, in which case the body is the container) Holy Oil causes Piercing 4 to Undead by Radiant.

fields.Note: May only be done in a place of religious power, not at a Hermetic Laboratory. name: Combustion Paint
materials: 1 Bloom, 1 Night Prize, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
process: This procedure can create unstable mixtures. Be sure to wear gloves, and use glassware that is free of cracks. Begin by filling a small glass bottle with Alchemical Suspension and adding the Essence Infusion. Then, being careful as to not disturb the solution, add the Bloom and Night Prize slowly, leaf by leaf to the solution. Cap and slowly heat this solution for one hourglass’s turn. Then leave the solution to cool for at least 30 seconds.
description: Black and sticky, like tar, with glimmers of metallic flakes suspended within the compound.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can do one blow that is “6 Flame” before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the alchemical mixture will become inert. If ingested, Combustion Paint causes Piercing 8 by Flame.

name: Liquid Agility
process: Boil some water in a container over a flame. Once it has a vigorous boil to it, slowly add in the leaves of the Bloom a little bit at a time, over the course of a few minutes. Continue to let it boil. Once all the Bloom has been added, add in the Rare Minerals. Quickly stir the mixture while removing it from heat. As it cools, it will turn into a silver liquid. Add two pinches of salt and 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension while continuing to stir. After a full turn of the hourglass while cooling, the mixture can be poured into a fresh vial to be used later.
description: Looking like quicksilver, this potion is usually shot quickly as it is believed to be good luck to drink it as fast as possible.
effect: Grants one Protect vs Thrown Weapons that lasts until the end of the Event or until used. The character should call “Protect” when they use this defense.

name: Malleable Beauty
process: Start by sprinkling a pinch of salt over the Bloom. Grind all of the Bloom into a fine powder with the mortar and pestle. Dust the mixture over the Night Prizes and heat them over a flame for one turn of the hourglass, keeping it a good distance from the flame so that it doesn’t burn, but instead just lightly toasts. The heat of the flame will activate the Night Prizes and infuse the powder dusted upon it. Gently shake the powder off the Night Prizes. The Night Prizes can be discarded and the powder will then be ready for consumption. The dust should be collected in a vial.
description: Malleable Beauty is a powder that is poured upon the tongue to dissolve. Most will let it naturally dissolve there as it takes effect. It has a strong sour flavor to it, which is why many eat it with honey.
effect: This Corrupted Alchemy creates a strong role-play effect. Part of that includes reduced inhibitions, a strong euphoric effect, and will encourage the user to try things that they wouldn’t normally do. In addition, it has the mechanical effect of allowing the character to recover one spent Power of Advanced, Basic, or Utility tier (the Power cannot have the Refresh Effect or a refresh time of greater than a Short Rest), or refresh one expended Novice or Adept spell-slot. The effects last approximately one hour or until the player wishes it to end.

name: Minor Mana Potion
process: Take the Bloom and wrap it entirely around the Night Prizes. Squeeze the two so that any liquid from the Night Prizes is soaked up by the Bloom. Then, heat the Bloom slowly by candle flame until warm to the touch, but not burning. While the Bloom is still hot, tear it into fine bits and soak in a vial with Alchemical Suspension. Shake for one hourglass turn.
description: Bright blue, this thick potion tastes sickly sweet when drunk.
effect: Instantly restores three Novice level spell-slots that have been expended. This potion causes Mana Sickness.

name: Pillowspore Poison
process: Take the Hide and shave with the knife, collecting the shavings that come off into a vial. Hold the Night Prizes in the tongs over flame until it burns, then put the ashes into a vial with six drops of Alchemical Suspension. Run the contents of the first vial (with the Skin shaving) through the retort into the second vial. Agitate for a turn of the hourglass, then heat for a turn. Then agitate for a turn. Leave to cool and then cork.
description: This hazy liquid smells slightly of soap.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that is “Short Sleep by Poison,” before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Pillowspore Poison will become inert. If ingested, Pillowspore Poison causes “Sleep by Poison.”

name: Ritual Powder
usesPerBatch: Creates one dose of Ritual Powder
process: Using the knife, shave down the Hides as much as possible. Grind the Bloom, Night Prize, and Harvest, in the mortar. Mix with water, adding drop by drop until a stiff paste is created. Let sit for three turns of the hourglass and then grind up into powder in the mortar.
description: This powder is used in Ritual magic and is a basic ingredient of those powerful magics. Multiple doses of this powder can be kept in a single container.
effect: Acts as a basic requirement for many Rituals.

name: Spellnaught Elixir
process: Using the scale, find a packet of materia that is as close to the same weight as your six Bloom. Drip 24 drops of Alchemical Suspension over the packet, and squeeze out the Alchemical Suspension into a vial. Grind all six Bloom into a fine powder with the mortar and pestle, then add to the materia-soaked Alchemical Suspension in a vial. Shake vigorously for at least 50 shakes. The Alchemical Suspension is not consumed in this process.
description: Smelling of cloves, the potion is a light auburn color.
effect: The potion, when drunk, Grants a Protect vs Packets.

name: Steel Oil
process: Harvest the Bloom specifically for any petals it has, and discard all other remains. Soak the petals completely in the Essence Infusion for at least an hour. After returning, decant Alchemical Suspension and keep (as a nice, floral flavored liquor, or for more alchemy in the future). Grind the soaked petals with the Ingots using the mortar and pestle for three turns, then put the resulting slurry into a vial.
description: A greasy thick oily paste, which after being exposed to air for a few moments, hardens and reinforces cracks.
effect: Upon roleplaying applying this oil to a piece of armor for at least a Quick 30, while this piece is worn, the character's Armor Points increases by one (both Maximum and Current Armor Points go up by one) until the end of the next Long Rest. Because of the nature of the oil, it stiffens, meaning that the armor piece must be worn when it is applied, and the effect will end if the piece of armor is removed. Steel Oil will work on non-metal armors, but not on summoned or natural armor. No single piece of armor can benefit from more than one Steel Oil at any one time. Steel Oil, if ingested, will cause the Slow by Poison Effect.

name: Trollblood Potion
materials: 6 Bloom, 10 Harvest, 1 vial of blood from any creature that was drained within the last hour
process: Slowly heat the vial of blood over a flame for ½ turn of an hourglass, or until the blood starts to coagulate. Grind the Harvest in a mortar and pestle; once it is the consistency of rough dirt or rock, start to add in the Bloom while continuing to grind. The total amount of time should be about 30 seconds. Take the mixture and add it to the vial of blood along with two drops of alcohol. Let sit for the time of a Short Rest before using.
description: A mixture of bone, blood and flower, it is an oddly chunky substance, with a smell of iron and floral overtones. The two do not mix well.
effect: The character can Heal 1 Life Point by Focusing for a Quick 100. They will Heal to full after any Rest. The character will have vulnerability to Flame and Acid, taking a Quick Drain Effect (as well as any other Effects) from them, and will generally have difficulty thinking quickly or deeply. This effect lasts until the end of a Long Rest.

name: Vision Valiant
materials: 6 Night Prizes, 3 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought
process: Scrape the Night Prizes’ surface for their skins, and add the skins to the mortar. Dispose of what remains of the Night Prizes to the Ashbin. While wearing gloves, slowly bruise the Night Prizes’ skins. At the same time, allow the Bloom to soak in the Essence Infusion. After three hourglass turns of bruising the Night Prizes’ skin, pour the Bloom-soaked water over them, and grind finely with a mortar and pestle for three turns. Pour the entire completed solution into a vial.
description: Salty and sweet, this milky white liquid goes down like candy.
effect: This Corrupted concoction lets the character see things usually unseeable. The character will be able to see both tracks (tracking flags: green with the White Halo marker) and the unseen (supernatural flags: red with a White Halo marker) and will be able to read any information cards on those flags. The enhanced vision will last only until the character takes a Long Rest.

name: Aerial Doom Powder
materials: 4 Night Prizes, 1 Rare Minerals, 5 Hide, 2 Harvest, 1 Alchemical Salts, 1 Ritual Powder, 1 Essence Infusion - Spirit OR Energy
process: For this recipe, it is important to make sure to cover your mouth and nose with a cloth soaked in water while making it, to try and protect your lungs. Keep the cloth damp throughout the procedure. For the first part of this recipe, boil the Rare Minerals in a bath of the Essence Infusion until the liquid of the Infusion has boiled off and it begins to smell burned. Grind the result into an extremely fine dust for three turns of the hourglass. Take the Night Prizes and soak it in water for about 30 seconds. Remove and squeeze out any liquid. Take what is left of the Night Prizes and chop finely. Grind up the two Harvest and add to the Night Prizes. Mix thoroughly. Add the ground Rare Minerals to the mixture. Stir it again and then spread it out over a cloth so that it dries. After about 10 minutes, coat a packet of materia in the powder.
description: A superfine gray powder, much like powdered graphite. A slight whiff of it burns the nostrils.
effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to deliver Death by Poison. If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill or the Journeyman Alchemist skill, that character takes the Effect. This powder is volatile and if held too close to materia activated with magic or other power, it will either go inert, if it is held while materia is charged, or will cause the other to go inert, if it is applied while there is activated materia in hand. Ingested, Aerial Doom Powder is actually surprisingly ineffective, doing “Piercing 2 by Poison.

name: Agony Elixir
materials: 1 Golden Blossom, 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Daggerpoint Cognac
process: The alchemist must provide a vial of heartblood. This is blood taken from as close to the heart as possible, preferably from deep inside the chest cavity. Opening the wound enough will require the loss of at least one Life Point and then two more Life Points worth of blood must be collected before any Healing is received. This is boiled quickly over an open flame and then the Essence Infusion is added and agitated.
description: This tastes like deep, rich blood. Regardless of how long ago it was brewed, it is warm, and the vibrancy of the liquid is obvious.
effect: This potion gives the drinker the ability to cast one spell with the Revive Effect, even if they would normally be unable to because of that spell’s once-per-Long-Rest limit. The spell to be cast may be any such spell, but only one additional casting is granted by the Agony Elixir. As soon as this potion is imbibed, the character is wracked with unbelievable pain, which feels like every ounce of blood in their body is trying to stab them with tiny shards of glass from the inside. The character is Inherently Drained by Agony until they complete a Short Rest. There are many who have braved one drink of this type for the common good and have then sworn to die before they take another sip, whatever the cost.

name: Caustic Glob
materials: 1 Night Prizes, 10 Hide, 2 Adderstrike Venom, 1 Hardening Lacquer
process: Take the Night Prizes and soak it in water for about 30 seconds. Remove and squeeze out any liquid. Take what is left of the Night Prizes and chop finely. Grind the Night Prizes in the Mortar and Pestle for a period of at least 60 seconds.  Stir it again and then spread it out over a cloth so that it dries. After about 10 minutes, brush the mixture onto the Hide and bind them together into a glob.
description: A toxic goop ready to be applied to some materia.
effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to cause Wounding 3 by Poison or Wounding 3 by Acid (Chosen by Alchemist at creation and written on card not on application). If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill or the Journeyman Alchemist skill, that character takes the Effect.

fields.Crafting Process: Take the Night Prizes and soak it in water for about 30 seconds. Remove and squeeze out any liquid. Take what is left of the Night Prizes and chop finely. Grind the Night Prizes in the Mortar and Pestle for a period of at least 60 seconds.  Stir it again and then spread it out over a cloth so that it dries. After about 10 minutes, brush the mixture onto the Hide and bind them together into a glob.
fields.Description: A toxic goop ready to be applied to some materia.
fields.Effect: By applying this to a packet of materia, it can be thrown to cause Wounding 3 by Poison or Wounding 3 by Acid (Chosen by Alchemist at creation and written on card not on application). If a character handles the packet without the Poisoner skill or the Journeyman Alchemist skill, that character takes the Effect.

name: Cobrastrike Venom
materials: 8 Night Prizes, 1 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 4 Nyte Tea
process: Pick apart the caps of the Night Prizes and place them in a vial. Cover just slightly with water and stir gently for a Quick 30. Chop up the stems with a sharp object and slowly add to the mixture. Let it boil for three full hourglass turns and then decant into a vial or jar.  Grind the Harvest and Rare Minerals in the Mortar and Pestle for 1 full hourglass turn then mix into the vial or jar. Deposit Night Prizes, Harvest, and Rare Minerals into Ashbin.
description: A yellowish brown liquid that slightly burns to the touch when applied to bare skin. It has a strong acidic smell to it.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack with a +4 bonus to its damage “by Poison” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Cobrastrike Venom will become inert. This may also be added to a drink and it will cause 10 points of Piercing damage by Poison.

name: Cure Raw Scale
materials: 2 Raw Scale, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Rare Minerals, 3 Hide, 2 Harvest, 3 Bloom, 1 Vial of Transformation
usesPerBatch: Refines Two Raw Scale into a single Cured Scale
process: Take the Night Prizes and Bloom and pull them apart and place them into a large beaker. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension as well as three vials worth of water. Heat over a flame for two turns of the hourglass. Slowly pour the Rare Minerals into the beaker, one at a time, making sure not to inhale any of the fumes. Chop the Harvest up into fine pieces and place on top of the Raw Scales. Let the mixture in the beaker cool for a few turns of the hourglass and slowly pour over the Raw Scales. This will release a toxic mist, so be prepared*. Set the Raw Scale out into sunlight for six hours for it to fully cure. *At this point in the process, you must make a ‘Pollution Call’ of “By My Voice 5 Piercing by Poison and Taint by Poison unless a Fume Hood is present (see Tinkering).
description: Using many rare and toxic resources, the Alchemist refines a Raw Scale into a Scale.
effect: This process takes a Raw Scale and turns it into a Cured Scale, an Advanced Resource.

name: Delicious Rage
materials: 5 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Weave, 1 Healing Draught
process: Draw two fingers worth of Alchemical Suspension into a standard vial. Place the Bloom in the mixture and heat over a flame for one full hourglass turn. Quickly pull from the flame and blow on the mixture to cool it quickly for a Quick 30. Add the Essence Infusion to the vial. Reheat the mixture for another full hourglass turn. Remove from and decant into a vial.
description: The thickness of blood, but dark purple in color, it has a spicy kick to it.
effect: This Corrupted Alchemy produces a dark liquid that will give the following benefits until the character completes a Short Rest: The roleplay effects of this alchemy is that it makes the inhibitor aggressive and irritable while under its effects.

name: Double Brew
materials: 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy
process: Take a vial of Alchemical Suspension, add three pinches of salt. Stir. Bring to a boil while adding five drops each of the two Essence Infusions (the rest should be discarded). Cork.
description: Suspended gold flakes float around in this faint blue liquid.
effect: When added as the last step to any alchemical recipe that is in the public list (with the exception of Double Brew), it creates two of those preparations rather than one. That means if you use Double Brew with Alcohol, it will produce 6 servings rather than 3 servings.

name: Elemental Protection Potion
materials: 3 Bloom, 1 Essence Infusion - Energy, 1 Salve of Shielding, 1 Alchemical Suspension
process: Draw two fingers worth of Alchemical Suspension into a standard vial. Place the Bloom in the mixture and heat over a flame for one full hourglass turn. Quickly pull from the flame and blow on the mixture to cool it quickly for a Quick 30. Add the Essence Infusion to the vial. Reheat the mixture for another full hourglass turn. Remove from and decant into a vial with Salve of Shielding.
description: Beautiful mixture of Yellow, Red, Blue, or Green depending on the element protected..
effect: Grants the drinkers a single Protect from either Flame, Ice, Lightning, or Acid.  The type of Protect granted is chosen by the alchemist at creation.  The batch may mix and match different accent types for the Protect between the four above, but a different card is needed for each type.

name: Eternal Ink
materials: 1 Bloom, 1 Adept Spell worth of Arcane Ink, 3 Rare Minerals, 2 Harvest, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought 5 Hide, 1 Instant Glue
usesPerBatch: Two Greater-tier spells worth of scrolls 1 Last BreathExpiration: Permanent
process: Take two pinches of salt and sprinkle over the Bloom. Using tongs, heat each Bloom for a few seconds over a candle flame, but not long enough to burn it. Crush the heated Bloom and mix with the Rare Minerals in a vial. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension. Next, break down the Harvest with the back of your knife. Add to a vial filled halfway with water. Boil for two turns of the hourglass. Remove from heat and slowly add to the Bloom/Rare Mineral mixture. Add water to fill the top of the vial. Let the mixture sit for 3 turns of the hourglass, then decant all the liquid into a vial containing the Essence and stir for one turn of the hourglass. Finally, discard all the non-liquid ingredients.  At a crafting station, the Alchemist should take the Hide and glue it together for 3 turns of the hourglass and mark their personal symbol on it.  This symbol can be anything the Alchemist chooses, but they must be consistent in the symbol they use.  (Do not mark the actual physical representation of Hide, instead place your symbol on the final item card.)This next process may take place anywhere, and does not need to occur at the Alchemy station.Take the Hide and place it over a recently deceased corpse that has not yet taken spirit form.  When the body dissipates and becomes a spirit, the Hide captures a small amount of the body’s life force and traps it in the Hide.  The Alchemist then should state, “Instruction to Spirit, Tell me your name.”  The Alchemist should write the creature’s name on the item card produced for Last Breath.  This has no obvious impact on the creature that has taken spirit form.
description: A purple-bluish ink with a slight silvery sparkle to it. A piece of hide with the Alchemist’s symbol on it.
effect: Eternal Ink is a specially crafted ink that Enchanters use for powerful inscriptions and can be used to scribe up to two Novice, Adept, or Greater-tier spells. See the Enchanting section for more information.Last Breath [Greater Alchemy Recipe] [BLOOD MAGIC] This crafted component is used in other things, totally legitimate I’m sure.  It has no obvious effects by itself.

name: Liberty
materials: 1 Night Prizes, 2 Bloom, 2 Nyte Tea, 2 Alcohol, Essence Infusion - Thought
process: Hold the Night Prizes and Bloom in a single bundle in the tongs. Roast over a fire lightly for two turns of an hourglass. Gently remove them from the flame and crush in a mortar to a coarse grit. After sunset (must be done within an hour of sunset), add the mixture to seven drops of Essence Infusion - Thought (the rest should be discarded).
description: Upon shaking and then released from the container, the liquid turns to gas and is inhaled with a faint smoky smell.
effect: By inhaling the fumes from this Corrupted Alchemy, the character is freed from fear of bondage. Until they complete a Long Rest, they will have Resistance to Bind, Charm, Dominate, Slow, and Root. In addition, they will be able to immediately escape from shackles and ropes. The Resistance is immediately lost if the character uses any of the Effects to which they are resistant.

name: Lifestrike Sunshine
materials: 1 Golden Blossom, 2 Holy Oil, Essence Infusion: Life
process: Take the Golden Blossom, grind it, and mix it into the Holy Oil. Then, heat it slowly over a candle flame for five hourglass turns while swirling. Once it begins to simmer, remove the vial, and allow it to cool. Now, either remove the Golden Blossom via decanting or straining the remaining liquid, from this liquid uncork the Essence Infusion: Life and add it to the liquid.  This will become the final product.  Deposit the materials in an ashbin.
description: A glowing white solution.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack which is “Revive to Undead by Radiant” with its next attack, before the end of the Event. If a second alchemical preparation of any kind is applied to the same weapon, the Lifestrike Sunshine will become inert. This may also be added to a drink or quaffed and it will deliver Revive to Undead Final.

fields.Note: May only be done in a place of religious power, not at a Hermetic Laboratory.IMPORTANT: An Alchemist may only create a single Lifestrike Sunshine per event unless another power supersedes this rule. name: Mana Potion
process: Take two Bloom and wrap entirely around a Night Prize. Squeeze the two so that any liquid from the Night Prize is soaked up by the Bloom. Discard the Night Prize in the Ashbin. Weigh the two Bloom on the scale with another two Bloom (it should be heavier with the Night Prize liquid). Add salts to the second two Bloom so that they are both equal in weight. Then, heat the two Bloom slowly by candle flame until warm to the touch but not burning. Tear up the last Night Prizes and Bloom and add to the First Bloom. While the Bloom is still hot, tear into fine bits and soak in a vial with Alchemical Suspension. Add the second two Bloom and salts to the vial. Shake for one hourglass turn.
description: Bright blue, this thick potion tastes sickly sweet when drunk.
effect: Instantly restores 3 spell slots of Adept tier or lower that have been expended. Drinking a Mana Potion causes Mana Sickness.

name: Noctilotoxin
process: Take the Harvest and cut it into small pieces/shards using the knife. Separate to one side. Slice the Night Prizes into pieces about the size of your small finger. Heat the pieces over a flame for a Quick 30. Remove from heat and grind in a mortar. Add three drops of Alchemical Suspension and continue to grind. It should start to form a paste at this point. Add in the chopped Harvest slowly while continuing to mix. Let rest for one turn of the hourglass and then carefully store it in a vial until use.
description: A green sticky substance that has a putrid odor, smelling of decay and rot. The smell is barely noticeable once on a weapon, but in its vial, if you hold it close to your nose it is very noticeable.
effect: Once applied to a weapon for a Quick 30, the weapon can make one attack that is “Taint by Poison'' before the end of the Event. If a second Alchemical preparation is applied to the same weapon, the Noctilotoxin will become inert. If ingested, Noctilotoxin causes “Taint by Poison, Silence by Poison.”

name: Overpotence
materials: 2 Bloom, 2 Harvest, 1 Essence Infusion - Spirit, 1 Daggerpoint Cognac, 1 serving of Alcohol.
process: This recipe has a few steps, but each one takes a good deal of time to finish. First, take the Harvest and grind in a mortar and pestle for a total of five turns of the hourglass. Take the Bloom and drip 3 drops of Alchemical Suspension over each piece. Let sit for approximately ten minutes. Combine all of the material with one serving of Alcohol. Mix over a low heat for three turns of the hourglass and then strain/decant out all materials other than the liquid. Let rest for 2 hours and then mix with the Essence Infusion of Spirit before corking.
description: Almost always taken with a chaser, this golden liquid has a strong flavor of ammonia which does not leave the mouth quickly.
effect: This potion gives the character 3 additional Base Spikes until the next Long Rest; this Alchemy cannot and does not stack with itself.  In addition, it Grants plus One Spike Bonus Damage that lasts until the end of their next Short Rest.

name: Steelskin Potion
materials: 2 Bloom, 2 Ingots, 1 Mithril Ore, 1 Essence Infusion - Life, 1 Essence Infusion - Thought.
process: First, put two Bloom each in four separate vials and add one quarter of the Essence Infusion - Life to each vial. Use the mortar to grind the Rare Minerals as finely as possible for at least one turn, then sprinkle a quarter of the dust into each vial of Bloom. Agitate each vial, cork, and let sit. Hold the vial of Essence Infusion - Thought over a flame for a Quick 30. Put one drop from each vial of Bloom into a fifth vial and fill the rest of the way with the Life Infusion. Cork.
description: A clear liquid. Close inspection will show whirls of metallic strings within the liquid. The taste will be strangely metallic as well.
effect: Grants three points of Bonus Natural Armor and gives Resistance to Weapon Damage. The character is Inherently Slowed. The effects of this preparation last only until the character completes a Short Rest.

name: Euphoria
materials: 6 Bloom, 1 Harvest, 1 serving of Alcohol, 1 serving of Nyte Tea, 1 Healing Draught
process: Chop the Harvest into tiny pieces with a knife. Put the pieces in a vial and hold over a flame until all are charred. Put Alchemical Suspension into that vial and agitate, then pour off the Suspension into another vial. Crush the Night Prizes with the mortar, and add to the charred Harvest. Add Alchemical Suspension and agitate again. Run the liquid through a retort and discard. Empty sediment from retort and mix with 6 drops of Alcohol. Heat for one turn. Cork and let cool.
description: Euphoria is more of a bitter paste that users smear onto their gums rather than a liquid that they drink.
effect: This paste causes a roleplay effect of extreme euphoria and physical well-being. Most who take Euphoria often feel more confident, attractive, and capable, and find themselves unworried by the possibility of failure, and other things that would normally cause them anxiety, shame, or fear. The character can ignore penalties from Indoor or Outdoor Discomfort and gains Resistance to Berserk and Fear Effects. These effects will last until the character completes a Long Rest, or until the player decides that the effect has worn off.

name: Realign Summoned Armor
process: The Enchanter should use a series of focuses to realign the target’s Summoned Armor for one turn of the hourglass. Such focuses may depend on the Enchanter or the target and may range from wands, sigils drawn on or around the targets, or other enchanting equipment found within the Enchanting Forge.
description: The Enchanter realigns the weave of the target’s Summoned Armor to revitalize its strength.
effect: After realigning the Summoned Armor of up to six individuals for one turn of the hourglass, the Enchanter may return a suit of Summoned Armor to its maximum armor points (this is a Mend Effect). Enchanters can always Mend any suit of Summoned Armor to maximum points by spending one turn realigning it.  If working on Summoned Armor created by an item they can also Rebuild Summoned Armor that has been previously Dispelled.

name: Bless Holy Symbol
materials: 3 Harvest, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Base Talisman, 1 Essence - Thought
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice with the Harvest and Night Prizes. Kneel or sit on the ground.  One at a time, take a Harvest and set it on the ground.  Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the Harvest and then recite the Chant of Sacrifice. Repeat with each Harvest and then each Night Prize, each time ringing the bell once and reciting the Chant. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin. Circle of Empowerment: Take the Base Talisman in both hands and hold aloft, reciting the Chant of Empowerment six times, each time increasing in volume.  Pour the Essence - Thought over the Talisman.  Make sure that the entire Talisman gets covered.  Touch the Talisman once with the piece of silver and recite the Chant of Empowerment once last time loudly.  Discard the Essence - Thought into the Ashbin.
description: This consecrates a Talisman with Divine power, causing the wielder to be able to heal those who may be injured more easily.
effect: A Cleric or Druid can use the Blessed Holy Symbol to grant themselves 1 additional Healing Touch / Life Tap per Cleric / Druid class-level. The Base Talisman used is not consumed in the process, but is instead augmented by this Enchantment, and carries with it all existing effects.

name: Copy Formula, Schematic, or Recipe
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Sacrifice the Hide in the Circle of Sacrifice and infuse it into the parchment. Hold the Hide in hand while sitting or kneeling and speaking the Chant of Sacrifice. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld. Rune Circle: Take a piece of parchment which is at least 5 1/2” x 8” and make the Chant of Sacrifice to release the soulheld Hide-essence into the paper. Then, using the Ink, copy the Formulae, Recipe, or Schematic, and sign your name to the bottom.
description: The enchanter takes a piece of parchment and uses Simple Ink to replicate an existing Schematic, Recipe, or Formulae that they can reference during the process, consuming the Simple Ink in the process.
effect: This creates an additional copy of the referenced Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe.

fields.Circle of Sacrifice: Sacrifice the Hide in the Circle of Sacrifice and infuse it into the parchment. Hold the Hide in hand while sitting or kneeling and speaking the Chant of Sacrifice. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld.

fields.Effect: This creates an additional copy of the referenced Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe.
fields.Note: Some Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe may have additional materials required to copy. And some may not be able to be copied at all. If this is the case, those alterations will be notated on the Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe. Any Formulae, Schematic, or Recipe in the rulebook does not need to be copied, as they are considered general knowledge available to all.

name: Enchant Patch Kit
process: Then, gather the power of the Harvest and Quartz within your spirit, and lift the assembled resources to your heart, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice twice. Bring the empowered Patch Kit to the Rune Circle. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
description: This is a small kit of materials preassembled to quickly patch up a suit of summoner armor. The kit represents a kit including items such as rune engraved leather strapping, small enchanted metal plates, mystic herbs, and the like. Can quickly be used to patch up a suit of summoned armor.
effect: This kit allows someone to patch up a suit of summoned armor with a Focus Quick 100. In order to patch the summoned armor, the player must roleplay as though they are actually fixing the armor. An Enchanter using such a kit will Mend 4 points of summoned armor. If a character without any Enchanting skill uses an Enchanted Patch Kit, it will Mend only 2 points of summoned armor. Either way, this uses up the kit.

fields.Circle of Sacrifice: Place the Patch Kit to be enchanted in the center of the Circle of Sacrifice, and arrange the Harvest in a line on top of the Patch Kit. Take the Quartz and place it below the Patch Kit. Then, gather the power of the Harvest and Quartz within your spirit, and lift the assembled resources to your heart, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice twice. Bring the empowered Patch Kit to the Rune Circle. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
fields.Rune Circle: Bring the Patch Kit to the Rune circle, focusing on magical words of power. Inscribe the word “MYSTIC” onto the Patch Kit in Primal Script, reciting the chant of Assignment three times as you do so. Finally, place the Enchanted Patch Kit on the ground in the middle of the rune circle and draw a circle with the silver to purify the last of the enchantment energies.

name: Enchant Sharpening Stone
materials: 20 Sharpening Stones, 20 Bloom, 5 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 1 Essence - Any
process: The Circle of Assignment: Take the piece of material that corresponds to the type of enchantment that you are performing.  Hold a sharpening stone in the left hand, the piece of material in the right.  Standing aloft, repeat the Chant of Assignment, once each sharpening stone layering the power over the Sharpening Stones.
description: A stone made of a fragile material. When a weapon is run across the edge it is quickly worn away but not until the object is sharpened. This has the additional effect of being enchanted, based on the Essence used.
effect: A character can use a Sharpening Stone to alter their Spike damage on one Spike they use before the next Short Rest, as well as the other effects listed below, based on the Essence used in the Enchantment.

fields.Crafting Materials Needed: 20 Sharpening Stones, 20 Bloom, 5 Harvest, 1 Rare Minerals, 1 Essence - Any
fields.Circle of Sacrifice: Bring the Bloom, Harvest and Rare Minerals into the Circle of Sacrifice. Place the Sharpening Stones in a pile in the middle of the circle.  Place the Bloom around the Sharpening stones.  Sit or kneel on the floor.  Take four Bloom in the left hand, one Harvest in the right hand.  Repeat the Chant of Sacrifice. Do this five times using up all the Bloom and Harvest.  Take the Rare Minerals and pour over a piece of material corresponding to the Essence that you are using for the enchantment. Deposit the Bloom, Harvest, Rare Minerals and Essence into the Ashbin. The Circle of Assignment: Take the piece of material that corresponds to the type of enchantment that you are performing.  Hold a sharpening stone in the left hand, the piece of material in the right.  Standing aloft, repeat the Chant of Assignment, once each sharpening stone layering the power over the Sharpening Stones.
fields.Description: A stone made of a fragile material. When a weapon is run across the edge it is quickly worn away but not until the object is sharpened. This has the additional effect of being enchanted, based on the Essence used.
fields.Effect: A character can use a Sharpening Stone to alter their Spike damage on one Spike they use before the next Short Rest, as well as the other effects listed below, based on the Essence used in the Enchantment.
fields.Note: A stone can be used on any weapon, not just edged - a fact that has confounded weapon crafters for as long as time itself. Any usage of a Sharpening Stone takes a Quick 100. Once it has been used, it is used up and needs to be discarded. The Sharpening Stone should be represented with some sort of smooth stone.

name: Enchant Source
materials: 2 Bloom, 1 Ingots, 2 Rare Mineral, 2 Hide, 1 Essence - Any
process: Next, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face east, take a piece of silver, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Then, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face north, take a piece of materia, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Finally, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face south, take a piece of glass, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Then take the synchronized vessel to the Circle of Assignment.
description: The item that is the target of this enchantment is imbued with elemental power, creating an Arcane Accent able to alter the Sourcerer Spells cast with it depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
effect: Each item may only be enchanted once. The next creature to hold the Arcane Source outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.

fields.Crafting Materials Needed: 2 Bloom, 1 Ingots, 2 Rare Mineral, 2 Hide, 1 Essence - Any
fields.Circle of Sacrifice: Place the vessel to be enchanted in the center of the Circle of Sacrifice, and arrange the Bloom, Ingot, and Hide in a circle around the vessel. Then crush and place the Rare Mineral on top of the vessel, allowing the powder to spill over the side. Finally, gather the power of the Essence within your spirit, and lift the vessel above your head, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice three times. Bring the harmonized vessel to the Circle of Empowerment. Discard the expended resources into the Ashbin.
fields.Circle of Empowerment: Holding aloft the harmonized vessel face west, then take a piece of quartz, and crush it with your free hand over the top of the vessel, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Next, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face east, take a piece of silver, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Then, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face north, take a piece of materia, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Finally, still holding aloft the harmonized vessel, face south, take a piece of glass, and burnish the vessel with it, and recite the Chant of Empowerment. Then take the synchronized vessel to the Circle of Assignment.
fields.Circle of Assignment: Sit down or kneel, and place the vessel in a bowl of sand in front of you. Recite the Chant of Assignment five times while touching the quartz to the vessel. Once that is complete, the next creature with Sourcerer class-levels to hold the vessel outside the Enchanting Forge will be attuned to it.
fields.Description: The item that is the target of this enchantment is imbued with elemental power, creating an Arcane Accent able to alter the Sourcerer Spells cast with it depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
fields.Effect: Each item may only be enchanted once. The next creature to hold the Arcane Source outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.

name: Enchant Weapon
materials: 1 Rare Mineral, 2 Harvest, 10 Ingots, 1 Essence - Any
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Place the Ingots around the circle. Standing in the middle, recite the Chant of Sacrifice ten times, one for each Ingot. Circle of Empowerment: Temper the weapon by holding it over a candle while reciting the Chant of Empowerment. Next, strengthen the weapon by pouring sand over it while reciting the Chant of Empowerment. Next, purify the weapon by pouring water over it while reciting the Chant of Empowerment. Finally, prepare the weapon for change by whispering the Chant of Empowerment over it. Take the weapon to the Circle of Assignment without letting it touch candle-flame, water, breath, or sand. Circle of Assignment: Lay hands upon the weapon and recite the Chant of Assignment six times.  Chant each time with increased mental pressure, layering the power of the Essence into the item, releasing the desired Essence from your soulhold. The weapon is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the weapon outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
description: The weapon is augmented to hum with an aspect of creation. The exact effect depends on the type of Essence used. Each Essence has multiple effects, and one effect must be chosen per item.
effect: Each item may only be enchanted once.

name: Scribe Novice Scroll
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Sacrifice the Hide in the Circle of Sacrifice and infuse it into the parchment. Hold the Hide in hand while sitting or kneeling and speaking the Chant of Sacrifice. The expended Hide should be dropped into the Ashbin while its essence is soulheld. Rune Circle: Take a piece of parchment which is at least 5 1/2” x 8” and make the Chant of Sacrifice to release the soulheld Hide-essence into the paper. Then, using the Ink, copy an Incantation from a Novice spell from a viewed spellbook onto the parchment. Just below that, write a copy of the Incantation in Primal Script.
description: The enchanter takes a piece of parchment and uses Simple Ink to scribe a magical scroll based off of a Novice spell in any Spellbook they can reference during the process, consuming the Simple Ink in the process.
effect: While the scroll is held in one hand (it need not be the only thing in the hand) and the Incantation on it is read aloud three times, the effects of the spell will occur as if produced by a spell-caster of the minimum level required to cast the spell. The effect of the spell cannot be altered from the base casting by any means. Characters with the Enchanting Skill need only read the incantation aloud twice. The reader must have at hand any material components required by the spell, such as Packets, weapons (if weapon-delivered), or the like. While reading, if the character is disturbed in any way that normal spell-casting would be disturbed (see Core Rules), the spell fails to cast, and the scroll is considered expended. Once the spell is cast, the scroll crumbles to dust, unless it would otherwise be refunded.

fields.Note: Effects that grant the Life Effect, and those that have the Target of Self are not scribable.

name: Harvest Essence
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice with the Harvest. Kneel or sit on the ground. One at a time, take a Harvest and set it on the ground. Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the 10 Harvest and then recite the Chant of Material Sacrifice. At the end of the entire process, place the Harvest reps in the Ash Bin and fill out the item card.
effect: Harvest Essence may be used as a direct substitute for almost anything that requires a Harvest such as Powers, Recipes, or other requirements. Of note, you may not use Harvest Essence to make additional Harvest Essence or other crafting that requires Harvest physreps. The yield of this recipe may not be altered under any circumstances.

Using a piece of silver, ring a bell over the 10 Harvest and then recite the Chant of Material Sacrifice. fields.Reality Tear: Ring the bell four times, each time waiting for silence before ringing it again. At that point, draw an Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the chapter). If you successfully draw the Spirit or Life Essence, place the Essence back and the process is complete. On any other draw including Unravelling, place the Essence back and begin the steps of the Reality Tear again.

name: Enchant Arcane Bracelet (Lesser)
materials: 3 Ingots, 2 Rare Minerals, 2 Night Prizes, 1 Essence - (1 of Energy, Spirit, or Weave)
process: Circle of Sacrifice: While sitting down in the center of the circle, light three candles around you. Take the three Ingots and pass them through one of the candle flames while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Take the 2 Rare Minerals and pass them through a different one of the candle flames while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Lastly, take the 2 Night Prizes and pass them through the last candle’s flame while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice. Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle and sit or kneel. Place the Essence on the ground before you, then create a triangle around it with the quartz, glass, and materia. Repeat the Chant of Assignment thrice, focusing your attention on the Essence; after each time you recite the chant, ring the bell. Pour the Essence over the material corresponding to the chosen Essence (quartz for Energy, glass for Spirit, materia for Weave), repeating the Chant of Assignment once more.Circle of Empowerment: Take the target and material corresponding to the chosen Essence into the circle. Place the target within the center of the circle, then stand aloft before the target, holding the material in both hands. Chant the Chant of Empowerment twice loudly, and focus the energy of the soulheld Essence into the target. After chanting with conviction and vigor, the now magical bracelet will be imbued with protective energies. The bracelet is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
description: Through this process, the enchanter imbues a non-magical bracelet with the ability to create summoned armor. The first person who wears the bracelet outside of the Enchanting Forge will attune themselves to it.
effect: When worn, this bracelet grants two points of summoned armor. The armor starts off exhausted (at 0 Armor Points) when the bracelet is first put on and the armor must be Mended before it is useful. The bracelet’s Summoned Armor may be mended to full by an Enchanter by spending a Slow 60 roleplaying re-forging the magical armor at the Enchanting Forge’s circle of Empowerment.

name: Enchant Gathering Implement
materials: 1 Essence - Any, 2 Harvest, 2 Night Prize, 2 Rare Mineral
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Sit or kneel within the circle with a single candle in front of you. Close your eyes, and recite the Chant of Sacrifice. Keep the chanting quiet enough that the flame of the candle in front of you is not blown out, repeating the chant three times. Once you have completed the chant, burn the Harvest, Night Prizes and Rare Minerals with the candle. Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (such as Quartz for Energy). Heat the material over the flame of a candle while reciting the Chant of Empowerment three times. Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle, standing up and facing north. Hold the target in one hand, and hold the material that corresponds to the type of soulheld energy being used in the other. Facing north, call forth the power of magic by reciting the Chant of Assignment. Focus and channel that energy from yourself into the target. Starting to the east, turn to the other three cardinal directions, reciting the Chant of Assignment each time. The target is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
description: The Gathering Implement is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing small effects depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
effect: The enchanter takes a piece of Essence and directly enchants a gathering implement with a single chosen effect from the following list. This will attune the implement to the wielder. Each item may only be enchanted once.

name: Enchant Glowstone
materials: Raw Radianite (special material), 1 Essence - One of [Spirit, Weave, or Energy]
process: Circle of Empowerment: Take the Radianite and place it within the center of the circle, then stand aloft before the Radianite. Strike a tuning fork against a piece of crystal, focusing the mind to find the exact moment of silence that happens after the tuning fork rings and slowly fade away; it often takes several rings to isolate the exact moment of ringing into silence. Once that moment has been found, clasp both hands together, chanting the Chant of Empowerment twice loudly, and focusing the energy of the soulheld Essence into the Radianite. After chanting with conviction and vigor, the Radianite will be transformed into Glowstone. Up to three pieces can be empowered at once. Circle of Assignment: Sitting or kneeling in the circle, repeat the Chant of Assignment thrice, once for each Essence soulheld. While doing so, hold aloft a piece of glass, materia, or quartz representing the Essence that are soulheld.
description: The discovery of Radianite was first thought to be good for nothing more than party tricks and late night Euphoria fueled dance parties. When researchers first realized its potential industrial applications, industry rapidly consumed every ounce of it they could get their hands on. Eventually, however, this led to a collapse in the supply - and as a result, the raw materials needed to craft Glowstones are rare to find.

fields.Crafting Materials Needed: Raw Radianite (special material), 1 Essence - One of [Spirit, Weave, or Energy]
fields.Circle of Assignment: Sitting or kneeling in the circle, repeat the Chant of Assignment thrice, once for each Essence soulheld. While doing so, hold aloft a piece of glass, materia, or quartz representing the Essence that are soulheld.
fields.Circle of Empowerment: Take the Radianite and place it within the center of the circle, then stand aloft before the Radianite. Strike a tuning fork against a piece of crystal, focusing the mind to find the exact moment of silence that happens after the tuning fork rings and slowly fade away; it often takes several rings to isolate the exact moment of ringing into silence. Once that moment has been found, clasp both hands together, chanting the Chant of Empowerment twice loudly, and focusing the energy of the soulheld Essence into the Radianite. After chanting with conviction and vigor, the Radianite will be transformed into Glowstone. Up to three pieces can be empowered at once.
fields.Description: The discovery of Radianite was first thought to be good for nothing more than party tricks and late night Euphoria fueled dance parties. When researchers first realized its potential industrial applications, industry rapidly consumed every ounce of it they could get their hands on. Eventually, however, this led to a collapse in the supply - and as a result, the raw materials needed to craft Glowstones are rare to find.

name: Enchant Hyperium
materials: 1 Essence - Any, 3 Ingots, 3 Harvest, 1 Refined Hyperium
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the Circle of Sacrifice and place the bar of Refined Hyperium in the center, with the six resources equally in a ring around it, alternating between Ingot and Harvest. Recite the Chant of Sacrifice thrice, each time walking counterclockwise around the circle, unwinding the magical impurities in the Ingots and Harvest, noting that they wither into dust. Deposit the expanded Ingots and Harvest ash into the Ashbin. Circle of Empowerment: Take the Purified Hyperium into the Circle of Empowerment, and strike it twice with the Resonance Fork to verify the harmonics of the crystals within. Quietly recite the Chant of Empowerment twice while infusing the energy of the Essence into the bar of Purified Hyperium, waiting until the resonance abates. Then strike the bar twice again, and recite the Chant again ensuring the bar has enough resonance to endure heating. Circle of Assignment: Take the Resonant Hyperium to the source of heat and let it bask in the flame for one recitation of the Chant of Assignment. Take care to avoid dropping the Hyperium ingot, as it now contains an exceptional amount of magical power, and is likely to shatter, rendering it worthless. Carefully quench the Resonant Hyperium in the basin of water, and let cool for one recitation of the Chant of Assignment. Once cooled, the ingot may be crumbled to dust in the hand and the crystals of Enchanted Hyperium pulled out. Discard the slag and ash, and collect the crystals.
description: One small bag worth of loose iridescent and translucent crystals known as Enchanted Hyperium.
effect: Enchanted Hyperium is used in the crafting of other, often magical items.

name: Enchant Shield
materials: 1 Essence - Any, 5 Hide, 5 Ingots, 5 Rare Minerals
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle and wrap one piece of Hide around an Ingot and Rare Mineral. Do this four more times. Take a piece of crystal and while peering through it, looking at each wrapped bundle, recite the Chant of Sacrifice once for each bundle. Circle of Empowerment: Take the target into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (such as quartz for Energy), and the Essence itself. Take the tuning fork and strike the target with it until the magical harmonies align. Once you feel that the pitch is right, say the Chant of Empowerment aloud three times while pouring the Essence over the material. The first time, chant loudly to wake up the soulheld energy within you. The second time, chant softly, focusing on the material that corresponds with the soulheld energy being used. The third time, chant with confidence, focusing on transferring the energy from you to the material. Circle of Assignment: Take a basin of water, the target, and the material into the circle. Place one hand into the basin and hold onto the material that corresponds to the enchantment with the other hand. Recite the Chant of Assignment. Move your hand from the basin to the front of the target. Repeat this process nine times (for a total of ten times) - placing your hand back in the basin before beginning the chant each time - focusing on transferring your energy to the target, letting the basin of water ground your magical energies each time. The shield is now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the target outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to it and may use it.
description: The shield is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
effect: The enchanter takes a soulheld piece of Essence and directly enchants a shield. This will attune the shield to the wielder. Each item may only be enchanted once.

name: Enchant Thrown Weapon
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Enter the circle with the targets, 3 Ingots, 2 Hide, and 1 Harvest. Touch each one to the targets while reciting the Chant of Sacrifice, once for each Ingot. Wrap the Harvest with both of the Hides. Char the outside of the Hide with the candle and rub the charred hide onto each target. Circle of Empowerment: Take the targets into the Circle. In addition, bring a piece of the material that corresponds to the Essence being imbued upon the weapon (quartz for Energy, for example). Start the enchantment by ringing a bell and reciting the Chant of Empowerment three times, each time focusing on the material and the soulheld energy. Circle of Assignment: Enter the circle and sit or kneel down, placing the targets in a bowl of sand in front of you. Hold aloft the targets in one hand and the material in the other. Recite the Chant of Assignment five times while touching the material to the targets, each time concentrating on transferring the power from the material to the targets. The throwing weapons are now ready. All resources used should be placed in the nearest ashbin. The next creature to hold the targets outside the Enchanting Forge will attune to them and may use them.
description: The weapon is imbued with raw magical energy, allowing a single chosen effect from the following list depending on the type of Essence used to perform the enchantment.
effect: The enchanter takes a soulheld piece of Essence and directly enchants a throwing weapon. This will attune the throwing weapon to the wielder. An attuned throwing weapon, if lost, will generally re-appear with the character (or close-by, such as at their Hearth or doorstep) at the beginning of the next Long Rest. The item will always have re-appeared in the character’s possession at the beginning of an Event. Each item may only be enchanted once. All three weapons must be wielded or thrown by the attuned user in order to gain the enchanted effects.

name: Delve Reality (Intermediate)
expiration: Lasts until the enchanter attempts to draw an Essence three times or leaves the Tear.
process: Circle of Sacrifice: Sitting or kneeling in the Circle of Sacrifice, hold the Bloom in the left hand and the Night Prize in the right. To sacrifice the Resources, repeat the Chant of Sacrifice nine times while holding the Resources and then deposit them into the Ashbin. Go to the Reality Tear.
description: The apprentice enchanter focuses their internal energy inward enough to be able to survive the process of reaching into a Reality Tear, retrieving a small piece of energy from the ether. The energy must be used quickly or otherwise preserved in an Essence Infusion. This Formula is intended for experienced Enchanters to have a greater chance of getting the specific Essence they want.
effect: The character can Draw from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section) up to three times. Each time, they can choose to keep the Essence they have chosen and soulhold it, or they may put it back. If they put it back, they can choose again, allowing up to three chances at a specific Essence the enchanter may want. Once they have chosen to soulhold an Essence, the Reality Tear closes and they cannot Draw another. If they draw an Unravelling Essence they immediately take a Death Final Effect.

fields.Reality Tear: Circle the Tear three times, each time ringing the bell. At that point, draw Essence from the Reality Tear (see Drawing procedure at the beginning of the section). For each draw attempt, circle the Tear three more times, ringing the bell again.
